description: Identify the highest-impact metrics, programs, and nodes in the learning/compliance graph. Uses causal centrality and topology to surface what matters most when resources are constrained.

question_patterns: what are the crown jewel metrics
if we can only watch five

transformations: compute centrality scores from causal graph topology
rank metrics by combined centrality (in_degree + out_degree)
identify terminal nodes (high in_degree = lagging indicators)
identify source nodes (high out_degree = leading indicators)
group by goal to show coverage across compliance objectives

cce_config.uses: centrality-based impact ranking — metrics with highest combined degree are crown jewels; topology drives leading/lagging classification

recommender_instructions.metric_selection_bias: prefer metrics with high causal centrality (in_degree + out_degree); deprioritize isolated metrics with no graph connections
recommender_instructions.output_guidance: each recommended metric MUST include: impact_rank (1..N), centrality_score, leading_or_lagging classification, and a 1-sentence business justification for why this metric is a crown jewel. Top 5 metrics should be flagged as 'must-watch'.
recommender_instructions.causal_usage: use csod_causal_centrality to rank all candidate metrics by combined degree; identify hub nodes that connect multiple goals; flag confounders and collider warnings

penalty_rules: penalize metrics with zero causal graph connections (-0.20)
penalize metrics that duplicate another metric's causal role (-0.10)
boost_rules: boost metrics that are causal hub nodes (degree >= 3) (+0.10)
boost metrics aligned with multiple goals (+0.08)
boost leading indicators with high out_degree (+0.05)
